question.text: An exporter buys 20 lots of USDINR 83 put at Rs. 0.075. The premium paid and premium received on squaring off at Rs. 0.04 gives a net loss of:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Premium paid = 20,000 × 0.075 = Rs. 1,500. Premium received on square-off = 20,000 × 0.04 = Rs. 800. Net loss = 1,500 - 800 = Rs. 700. **The Trap:** 20 lots × 1000 = 20,000 units. Don't forget the lot multiplier. **Cross-Exam Context:** Square-off P&L calculation is from the workbook example. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Squaring off before expiry recovers some time value, reducing the total loss compared to holding till an OTM expiry.
